events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: backstabbed device on 20A circuit. TRD says "legal but journeymen won't." NEC 110.14 requires terminations be properly made. Backstabbing (push-in) on 20A circuit is generally not allowed for 20A circuits because the device rating and termination method. Actually, backstab connections are typically only permitted on 15A circuits, not 20A. Most receptacles rated 20A with backstab are only rated for 15A backstab. Let me think.

NEC 110.14 requires electrical connections to be tight and suitable for the conductor. Backstabbed devices on 20A circuits are generally considered non-compliant because the push-in terminals on standard receptacles are only listed for 15A branch circuits. The device may be rated 20A but the backstab is only for 15A. So this would be non-compliant.

The TRD note says "legal but journeymen won't" — but actually for 20A circuits, backstabbing is not legal. The backstab terminals are only listed for 15A. So this is non-compliant.

Decision: fail. Risk: medium (potential for loose connection, arcing, fire). Component conditions: non-compliant, possibly installed-defective.

Let me write the JSON.
